title: DJ Set Planning & Flow
description: Think like a DJ for a full set — plan your energy arc, key your tracks, and build a sequence that tells a story from first to last track.

steps.instruction: Watch: a 3-track mini-set. Track 1 at medium energy. Track 2 building higher. Track 3 at peak energy.
Watch: each transition happens at a phrase boundary. The BPMs climb slightly with each track.
Watch: the third transition brings a new key and fresh energy. The journey from start to finish has an arc.

phases.instruction: Your set is a movie. The first track is the opening scene — a hook, not your biggest moment. You build through acts, rising tension, delivering peak moments at the right time, then releasing into a smooth close. Great DJs plan this arc. They know which track comes after which, and why.

steps.instruction: Start a 3-track mini-set. Load Track 1 on Deck A — a medium energy track. Play it at 80% volume.
Select Track 2 for Deck B — ideally at the same or slightly higher BPM, compatible key. Load and beatmatch it.
Transition from Track 1 to Track 2 using the EQ bass swap technique at a phrase boundary.
Fade Track 1 out completely. Track 2 is now the main track. Notice the energy level — higher?
Load Track 3 on Deck A — your peak energy track. Choose something that will feel like an upgrade from Track 2.
Beatmatch Track 3 to Track 2. Execute the final transition — the peak of your mini-set.
Make the transition from Track 2 to Track 3. Use whatever technique feels right — bass swap, drop mix, or filter transition.
Fade Track 2 out. Track 3 plays solo at full energy. You have just played a 3-track set with an energy arc.
